status_notes: Prototype taxonomy. Registry entry currently provides identity + resolution only; per-control data (CVSS attenuation, CWE relationships, verification commands) lands in a future Data layer (V2).
notes: CME is a structured taxonomy of defensive security controls (e.g. seccomp, ASLR, SELinux, MFA). Unlike CVE (vulnerabilities) or CWE (weakness classes), CME enumerates the mitigations, and maps each control to a deterministic CVSS environmental-score attenuation and to the CWE weakness classes it addresses. Tactics are D3FEND-aligned (Harden, Isolate, Detect, Evict, Restore). Prototype by J. West (2026); ~109 entries at time of registration. The namespace github.com/stuwrtlttle currently represents the CME project.

urls.note: Human-browsable static index (by CWE, by tactic, search)

match_nodes.description: Common Mitigation Enumeration — D3FEND-aligned taxonomy of defensive controls mapped to deterministic CVSS environmental attenuation

data.description: Defensive control taxonomy. Each CME-ID identifies a mitigation (e.g. CME-601 = kernel-level syscall filtering / seccomp) carrying the CVSS base metrics it modifies, the CWE classes it mitigates, machine-executable verification commands, and platform applicability.
data.notes: ID ranges are allocated per category (CME-100–199 Kernel Hardening, CME-200–299 Network Isolation, CME-300–399 Mandatory Access Control, CME-400–499 Cryptographic Controls, CME-500–599 Filesystem Hardening, CME-600–699 Syscall & BPF Controls, CME-700–799 Container & Privilege Isolation, CME-800–899 Credential & Identity Hardening, CME-900–999 Network Protocol & Application Controls, CME-1000–1099 Detection & Monitoring, CME-1100–1199 Eviction & Patch Management, CME-1200–1299 Restore & Recovery, CME-1300–1399 Application Input Validation).

children.description: CME control — raw JSON record (structured, source of truth)
